task: #61 Benchmark and iterate qwen35moe GGUF 512/128 prefill to PARO range
summary: 512/128 qwen35moe GGUF remains blocked far below PARO/llama.cpp-class prefill. Current correctness-safe raw fast-bulk default is stable at 99.920 tok/s; opt-in expert sidecar reduces MoE kernel time but worsens wall prefill to 62.400 tok/s because the 24 GiB card cannot keep raw GGUF weights plus full sidecars resident. rocprof shows raw prefill is dominated by row-wise GGUF GEMV kernels (Q8 dense plus selected experts), not attention.

hardware.gpu: attached RX 7900 XTX / gfx1100 in this session; project default claims remain W7900/gfx1100 unless separately verified

raw_default_512_prefill.interpretation: Raw default prefill wall time is almost entirely device kernel time. Q8_0 dense row-wise prefill, selected expert row-wise GEMVs, and GDN recurrent kernels account for nearly all kernel time; full attention is under 1%.

sidecar_512_prefill.interpretation: Sidecar kernels cut selected-expert device kernel time substantially, but prefill wall time worsens because transient sidecar allocation/copy dominates outside kernel time. Raw Q8_0 dense prefill then becomes the largest remaining kernel family.

rejected_iteration.name: gguf_k_gemv default threads 128 -> 256
rejected_iteration.reason: Rejected before throughput benchmarking because deterministic qwen35moe probe changed model output even though serial/default bulk parity stayed internally exact; this violates public fixture stability expectations.

blocker.summary: The current qwen35moe GGUF path is architecturally row-GEMV-bound. 512 tokens require hundreds of row-wise GGUF GEMV launches where each (row,out_col) dot product is a separate block. The profile shows ~4.37 s of raw default prefill kernel time in Q8_0 dense plus selected expert row-GEMV alone, already >17x the 0.256 s wall budget for 2000 tok/s. Expert sidecars solve part of MoE compute but not dense Q8_0 matmul and cannot stay resident beside raw weights on a 24 GiB device.
required_next_work: Implement/port matrix-tiled GGUF Q8_0/Q5_K/Q6_K prefill kernels or a dequantize+GEMM path with a memory plan; Q8_0 dense is 37.8% of raw prefill kernel time and 61.9% after sidecar.
Implement grouped-by-expert/tiled selected-MoE kernels that reuse packed expert rows across tokens instead of per-output row GEMVs.
Replace transient sidecar H2D copies with a residency policy that does not keep duplicate raw+packed expert weights, or add equivalent sidecar decode fallback so raw experts can be omitted.
Keep deterministic qwen35moe fixture gates when changing reductions; a 256-thread reduction probe changed token 4469 -> 451 and was rejected.
